label: Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)

node.label: Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO)
node.definition: GRPO is a PPO-style policy update that estimates relative advantages from groups of sampled responses instead of learning a separate value model.
node.why_it_matters: It reduces critic-memory overhead and fits reasoning tasks where several candidate completions can share one verifiable reward rule.
limitations: Training depends on within-group reward variation, sampling diversity, clipping stability, and resistance to reward hacking.

dependencies.rationale: In this atlas graph, Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives because Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.

In this atlas graph, Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) because Preference optimization (RLHF, DPO families) is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.

In this atlas graph, Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Outcome verifiers & executable rewards because Outcome verifiers & executable rewards is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.

In this atlas graph, Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) has a `uses` dependency on Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning because Supervised fine-tuning & instruction tuning is modeled as a reusable ingredient for the node.

sentence_units.text: GRPO is a PPO-style policy update that estimates relative advantages from groups of sampled responses instead of learning a separate value model.
It reduces critic-memory overhead and fits reasoning tasks where several candidate completions can share one verifiable reward rule.
Training depends on within-group reward variation, sampling diversity, clipping stability, and resistance to reward hacking.

In this atlas graph, Group Relative Policy Optimization (GRPO) has an `optimizes_for` dependency on Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives because Policy-gradient & actor–critic objectives is modeled as the objective family for learning, selection, or evaluation.
